label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this question. The passage is about folk medicine, and I need to figure out which of the given statements is true based on the text. Let's go through each option one by one. First, the passage starts by saying, "Folk medicine keeps the body healthy by using medicines taken from surrounding plants and herbs." That sounds exactly like option A: "It keeps one healthy by using medicines taken from plants and herbs." So A seems correct at first glance, but I should check the other options to be sure. Option B says, "It can treat most kinds of diseases for both man and animals." The passage mentions that animals use plants and herbs, and humans learned from them, but does it say it can treat "most kinds of diseases"? The text doesn't specify that it's effective for most diseases, just that it's used. Also, the passage later mentions that some stories about folk medicine are just fairy tales, which might imply that not everything is effective. So B might not be correct. Option C claims, "It is better than other medicines in curing serious diseases." The passage doesn't compare folk medicine to other types of medicine in terms of effectiveness. It just describes its historical use and origin. There's no mention of it being better, so C is probably not the right answer. Option D states, "It was a way of body treatment used only in man's early history." However, the first paragraph says, "one can practise folk medicine in any place of the world," suggesting it's still used today. Also, the passage says the use goes back to earliest man but doesn't say it was only used then. So D is likely incorrect. Going back to A, the passage clearly states that folk medicine uses plants and herbs to keep healthy. Even though
